title: Boykinia turbinata
taxonomy: Boykinia turbinata is a species of flowering plant in the family Saxifragaceae. It is a perennial herb native to eastern North America. The classification of Boykinia turbinata is within the genus Boykinia, which is part of the Saxifragaceae family. This plant is a member of the order Saxifragales and the class Magnoliopsida. The classification of Boykinia turbinata reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the plant kingdom.

intro: Boykinia turbinata, also known as the Mountain Everlasting, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Saxifragaceae family, characterized by its unique, tubular inflorescences and edible leaves. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as arthritis and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Boykinia turbinata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, which may be used to treat various ailments, including fever, skin irritations, and respiratory issues.
